title: rosa coriifolia var. subcanina
intro: Rosa coriifolia var. subcanina, also known as the Himalayan rose or Rosa subcanina, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rosa coriifolia var. subcanina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, making it a valuable herb for treating various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote overall well-being and immune function.
